title: HP Pre-Summit Analyst Phone Call — Kastner Feedback
author: Peter S. Kastner (Aberdeen Group, Chief Research Officer)
abstract: Transcript of a pre-summit analyst phone call in which HP sought Kastner feedback on corporate messaging, Adaptive Enterprise positioning, divisional execution (PSG, IPG, ESG, Services), and consumer/SMB/enterprise capabilities. Kastner delivers harsh ratings: 'low TCO' message not believable; Adaptive Enterprise too IT-facing; HP-Compaq brand confusion in retail; mobility late to market; strong in digital imaging and printing.
importance_rationale: Unfiltered primary-source snapshot of leading-analyst HP critique in real time; rare oral-history document capturing both the questions HP was asking and Kastner's verbatim assessment.
relevance_rationale: Framework (corporate messaging discipline, brand-portfolio management, IT-vs-business executive alignment) applies directly to 2025 enterprise-vendor strategy debates; HP's PSG/IPG/ESG split still informs today's HP/HPE separation.
prescience_rationale: Kastner's skepticism on Adaptive Enterprise, HP-Compaq brand confusion, and HP's trouble generating new business beyond installed base all proved accurate over the 2004-2015 period (eventual HP-HPE split in 2015).
